target: WIN-064 defense neutralization — preempt the 'disc not incompatible with shadow zone' retreat (non-contradiction framing) rated defense_survives=3 by curmudgeon c3 advocate. Appends ONE new paragraph to detail_extra; existing detail_evidence and detail_extra content remain unchanged.
insertion_point: In data/wins.json WIN-064.detail_extra, APPEND the new paragraph at the end of the field, immediately AFTER the existing final sentence '...but a model that is merely not-incompatible with an observation makes no prediction, and the dome's own scoreboard counts this as a confirmation, not a non-contradiction.' The existing 'Preempting the radially symmetric structure defense' paragraph stays in place as paragraph 1 of detail_extra; the new paragraph becomes paragraph 2.
insertion_anchor_tail: but a model that is merely not-incompatible with an observation makes no prediction, and the dome's own scoreboard counts this as a confirmation, not a non-contradiction.
insertion_html: Closing the "non-incompatibility" retreat (preemptive). The strongest remaining dome rebuttal is to step back from mechanism and argue that WIN-064 was never a derivation claim — the disc cosmology is simply not incompatible with the observed shadow zone, so a confirmation is warranted whether or not any disc geometry produces 104°–140°. This retreat is unavailable while the scoreboard reads as currently written, and ineffective even if it is taken. Unavailable: the public WIN-064 entry commits to a specific causal mechanism — "the Sub-Terrestrial Wall of the Closed Toroidal Ovoid creates a physical P-wave shadow zone between 104° and 140°… corresponding to the toroidal return-path boundary," and the Dome-vs-Globe row explicitly re-asserts the mechanism as a physical structural boundary … where the aetheric return path meets the bottom plate of the ovoid cavity. "Creates" is a production claim, not a compatibility claim; invoking non-incompatibility would require the author to publicly strike the verb, delete the mechanism sentence, and demote the CONFIRMED badge to "not logically forbidden by our geometry." Ineffective: the dome's own methodology, quoted verbatim on the live-power dashboard, states Each domain has explicit tolerance bands. If prediction falls outside tolerance, it fails — no exceptions. A non-incompatibility claim has no tolerance band by construction — every observation is compatible with "maybe, maybe not" — so every observation passes. That is already visibly the state of this panel (Tolerance: N/A, Observed in kilometres of focal depth while Predicted is in degrees of angular distance, Status: PASS regardless). By the dome's own self-imposed criterion, the retreat converts this domain — and, symmetrically, every other N/A-tolerance domain — into an unfalsifiable free-PASS that should not count toward the accuracy denominator. The dome cannot hold the "94.5% accuracy" figure and the non-incompatibility framing simultaneously: either WIN-064 carries a quantitative prediction with a tolerance (in which case the depth-vs-angle category mismatch and missing tolerance are unresolved failures), or it is a compatibility statement (in which case it is withdrawn from the confirmed count along with every other N/A-tolerance domain in the same pipeline). The same disjunction closes the fallback reading of Tolerance: N/A = deterministic yes/no: a genuinely qualitative test would omit the numbers and accept a shadow at any angle as confirmation, but the dome keeps the 104°–140° boundary on the scoreboard. You cannot ask for quantitative credit and qualitative falsifiability immunity on the same line.
holes_addressed: advocate_mode.best_defense leg (1) rated defense_survives=3 — non-incompatibility framing (dome claims disc not incompatible with shadow zone, therefore confirmation stands)
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation #1 — make explicit that non-incompatibility has zero predictive content, and the scoreboard counts it as confirmed not as non-contradiction
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation #3 (secondary) — closes the Tolerance: N/A = deterministic qualitative reading by showing the dome keeps the quantitative 104°–140° numbers on the scoreboard
new_evidence_added: Dome public WIN-064 text verbatim: "Sub-Terrestrial Wall of the Closed Toroidal Ovoid creates a physical P-wave shadow zone between 104° and 140°" — a production-verb mechanism claim incompatible with a later non-incompatibility retreat

Dome Dome-vs-Globe row on wins.html (verified 2026-04-21): dome publicly attributes shadow zone to "a physical structural boundary — the Sub-Terrestrial Wall where the aetheric return path meets the bottom plate of the ovoid cavity," doubling down on mechanism

Dome live-power "HOW THIS WORKS" quote (raw-text/08-live-power.txt): "Each domain has explicit tolerance bands. If prediction falls outside tolerance, it fails — no exceptions" — self-imposed falsifiability standard the P-wave panel does not satisfy (Tolerance: N/A)
Depth vs angle unit mismatch on the live panel line: Predicted "Shadow at 104-140°" vs Observed "Confirmed at depth 378 km" — angular phenomenon reported as a kilometres-of-focal-depth scalar

anticipated_objections.objection: Dome defender: 'The wins.html mechanism sentence is rhetorical flourish; the core claim is still just that the observation exists and the disc is not incompatible with it.'
anticipated_objections.response: Then the 'rhetorical flourish' is precisely the claim we are reviewing — the scoreboard text is what the dome chose to publish. A reviewer cannot be expected to discount the published mechanism in favour of an unstated weaker reading. If the author wants to disown the Sub-Terrestrial Wall sentence, the fix is to edit wins.html and demote the badge; until then, 'creates a physical P-wave shadow zone' is on the record and must be defended as a mechanism claim.

anticipated_objections.objection: 'Tolerance: N/A is standard practice for categorical predictions in many sciences; you are holding us to a stricter standard than is reasonable.'
anticipated_objections.response: Standard practice for categorical predictions is to omit numeric ranges from the prediction column, because there is nothing to compare. The dome keeps '104-140°' in the Predicted column. That is a quantitative prediction by definition — matching a tolerance band is how it would be validated. The stricter standard is not ours; it is the one the dome wrote into its own HOW THIS WORKS section.

anticipated_objections.objection: 'Non-incompatibility claims are a legitimate category of scientific confirmation — e.g., Einstein\'s theory is consistent with Mercury\'s perihelion; why cannot the dome claim the same status?'
anticipated_objections.response: Einstein's theory derives Mercury's perihelion shift quantitatively (43 arcsec per century) from first principles; the confirmation is that the derived number matches the observation. The dome has no analogous derivation of 104°–140° from disc_radius, firmament_height, or any other dome parameter. A model is 'consistent with' an observation only when it produces the observation; otherwise every theory is 'consistent with' every observation it does not explicitly forbid, and the category becomes vacuous.

anticipated_objections.objection: 'The panel\'s Observed field shows focal depth because the seismic record being live-fetched is a recent deep earthquake — the depth is a side-product, not the observed shadow zone value.'
anticipated_objections.response: That is precisely the point. The live panel is not actually observing the shadow zone; it is observing something else (a recent deep quake's focal depth) and labelling the panel PASS by convention. A panel that cannot display the quantity it claims to predict is a cosmetic indicator, not a validator — the definition of 'monitoring: hardcoded' (or worse) rather than 'live comparison.'

kernel_of_truth: The advocate is right in one narrow sense: non-incompatibility is a perfectly legitimate epistemic status for a theory to occupy with respect to a large body of evidence, and it is not intrinsically damning. General relativity is non-incompatible with the existence of the Moon, and we do not count that as a mark against GR. Where the defence breaks down is the asymmetry between how non-incompatibility is normally treated in science (implicit background, not on the scoreboard) and how the dome treats it (explicitly listed as 'CONFIRMED', counted in the accuracy denominator, accompanied by a mechanism sentence). The dome's author is entitled to claim non-incompatibility; he is not entitled to claim non-incompatibility AND credit on the scoreboard AND a causal-mechanism sentence all at once. Acknowledging the kernel — non-incompatibility is a real status — is what lets us show the escape is only available at the cost of voluntarily giving up the very counting convention that makes the 94.5% figure possible.

defense_neutralization.notes: Agree with curmudgeon rating. The non-incompatibility retreat is rhetorically strong because it trades a weak-but-unconditional concession (the dome 'explains' nothing about 104°–140°) for immunity to the azimuthal-symmetry refutation. The preemptive paragraph closes it by pinning the retreat to two testable public commitments: (a) the wins.html mechanism sentence 'creates a physical P-wave shadow zone' — a production verb, not a compatibility verb — which the author would have to publicly delete; (b) the dome's own live-power 'HOW THIS WORKS' quote requiring explicit tolerance bands, which WIN-064's panel does not have. The paragraph is an append, not a rewrite — the existing radial-symmetry preempt paragraph is unchanged. The Tolerance=N/A=deterministic defence is closed at the end of the same paragraph via the symmetry that the dome keeps the quantitative 104°–140° boundary on the scoreboard. Does NOT address curmudgeon hole #1 (stale code_analysis.monitoring tag) — that was already flipped from 'none' to 'hardcoded' in the current wins.json and the detail_verdict_text, and does not require analyst action; decider may want to confirm Part 2b aggregate counts are re-computed.
